Patient decision room

The clinic can lose the patient before anyone inside knows they existed.

Patients do not experience the clinic's internal quality first. They experience visible options, local signals, doctor familiarity, confidence, and booking friction.

INTERACTIVE DECISION PATHTap a stage. The patient logic updates instantly.
01Active patient stage
What the patient sees first

Visible options—not internal quality.

MapsLocal discoverability
ReviewsVisible confidence
Service intentTreatment relevance
If the clinic is not visible in the right places, the patient never reaches the next stage.
Competitor pressure map

The patient does not compare you in theory. They compare visible options in the same area.

The personalized version receives the clinic logo, real area, and 4–6 nearby competitors. This turns a vague marketing discussion into a visible local decision environment.

Primary lens · Booking & capacity
CGD / PRIVATE

Booking demand may be leaking before capacity is properly used.

The first leadership question is not “How many leads can we buy?” It is “Where does qualified intent disappear between discovery, response, booking, attendance, and repeat value?”

Visible signal→Commercial implication→Leadership question
[Evidence A]clinic-specific screenshot or signal
[Evidence B]clinic-specific commercial implication
Revenue opportunity model

From seasonal spikes to higher-quality profit.

At stronger utilization, profit quality can improve because many base costs are already paid. Fixed costs may rise, but not necessarily in the same proportion as revenue.

Existing physical investment

Doctors, staff, rooms and devices are already carrying cost.

ONE OPERATING BASETwo utilization outcomes — compare them in one glance.
Current state model
~35%
Cost base
Already heavy
Patient flow
Inconsistent
Profit quality
Limited
→
Stronger utilization model
~75%
Cost base
Slightly higher
Patient flow
Structured
Profit margin
32–40%
Expansion readiness

Saturate the current operation before expanding the footprint.

Stable demandStronger utilizationMeasured pressureExpansion discussion

This is a strategic path, not a guarantee. Expansion becomes a rational discussion only when demand, trust, capacity, and performance are stable enough to support it.

Year-round booking consistency

The goal is not one strong campaign. It is consistent monthly bookings.

Obvious seasons create spikes. Serious growth builds a calendar of high-intent moments—then connects those moments to doctor authority, offers, booking control, and capacity.

Weddingsplanned premium journeys
Graduationconfidence-driven demand
Traveldeadline-based care
Maintenancerepeat value programs
Engagementsfamily visibility moments
Smile transformationhigh-intent decision
Skin resetstructured program
Family occasionslocal demand rhythm
12-MONTH DEMAND RHYTHMPlanned moments replace isolated spikes.
JANFEBMARAPRMAYJUNJULAUGSEPOCTNOVDEC
Local trust infrastructure

Doctor authority is not random content.

It is a structured path from repeated value to local patient preference.

01
FamiliarityRepeated, useful visibility
02
TrustClear explanations and safety
03
Preference“I want that doctor”
04
BookingLower uncertainty at decision time
The goal is not empty fame. It is to become the obvious trusted choice in the area where patients can actually visit and book.
Premium demand angles

High-value demand becomes easier to understand when the patient moment becomes visible.

These examples package occasion, urgency, treatment logic, perceived value, and a clear reason to book—without reducing the clinic to discount marketing.

Revenue opportunity model

The math of what can be recovered.

Adjust the assumptions with leadership. This is an opportunity model, not a guarantee. Final numbers depend on booking quality, capacity, average patient value, service mix, patient experience, cooperation, and execution quality.

10
$760
26
Monthly opportunity$198K
Annual opportunity$2.37M
Cost of no decision

Waiting does not preserve the current position. It keeps the leakage running.

No action is still an operating decision. The clinic continues carrying fixed costs, patient opportunities remain untested, competitors keep compounding visibility, and leadership delays the evidence required for the next move.

Phased exposure curveRamp-up first · compounding pressure later
3MFoundation delayed 6MLearning delayed 12MCompounding deferred
Illustrative opportunity left untested $593K Across 3 months of delayed action
Patient opportunities delayed 780

Illustrative additional booking opportunities not yet tested.

Fixed-cost pressure continues Every month

Doctors, rooms, staff, devices, and operations remain active.

Authority compounding postponed 3 months

Doctor familiarity, trust, reviews, and patient memory do not pause.

Competitor learning continues 3 cycles

Other clinics keep testing offers, channels, and patient messages.

This is not a claim of guaranteed lost revenue. It is a transparent decision lens based on the assumptions selected in the opportunity model above.

The value is not any single channel. The value is the operating connection between demand, booking, experience, retention, authority, measurement, and the next decision.

Patient value compounding

One patient can become one transaction—or a long-term growth asset.

Acquisition opens the relationship. Experience, intelligent follow-up, repeat value, referrals, and doctor authority are what make growth compound.

Disconnected path

Acquire. Treat. Go silent. Buy attention again.

Attention
→
Booking
→
Treatment

The relationship ends too early, so the clinic repeatedly restarts from zero.

Compounding path

Acquire once. Build value across the relationship.

Qualified booking
→
Experience
→
Retention

The patient can return, take the next relevant treatment, refer others, and strengthen trust.

Digital-powered strategic team

Modern tools accelerate the work. Human judgment controls the direction.

AI accelerates research, clinic-specific analysis, briefs, testing, and quality control. Strategy, medical trust, leadership communication, and final decisions remain human-controlled.

DIGITAL-POWERED TEAM
One team · one direction

Not a chain of disconnected tasks.

A multidisciplinary operating team studies the clinic, aligns priorities, directs execution partners, and keeps every channel connected to bookings, capacity, patient value, and the next decision.

Market intelligenceCreative strategyDoctor authorityBooking systemsPatient valueAnalytics & decisions
Shared growth responsibility

A growth system needs a clinic ready to deliver.

Sustainable growth is created when the growth system and the clinic experience reinforce each other.

SELECTIVE PARTNERSHIP

The right coverage is defined after the clinic's real growth scope becomes clear.

CGD partnerships are shaped around the clinic's operating complexity, capacity, service structure, leadership ambition, and the level of execution required.

The purpose of this private review is to establish whether there is a genuine strategic fit before defining the appropriate coverage.

Annual strategic partnership Milestone-based activation Limited clinic selection Shared execution responsibility
The next step is not choosing a package. It is confirming the right mandate.
Review the fit with us
15-day mobilisation

The first payment activates controlled preparation—not random activity.

Our digital-powered strategic team uses the first 15 days to study the clinic's specific market, booking behaviour, capacity, patient experience, authority opportunity, and launch priorities before controlled coverage begins.
